topic: The government should ban single-use plastic bags

systemArguments.text: Plastic bags are convenient for shopping
People will just use other plastic items instead
Stores will have to buy new bags
Customers might forget to bring reusable bags
Government shouldn't control what people use

goldenArguments.text: Individual freedom includes the right to choose convenient products
Government regulation should not interfere with personal consumer choices
Free market principles should determine product availability
Plastic bag bans increase costs for businesses and consumers
Alternative materials may have worse environmental impacts than plastic
Bans create enforcement burdens and regulatory complexity
Consumer behavior change should be voluntary, not mandated
Plastic bag production supports manufacturing jobs and economic activity

humanRationale: System arguments are overly simplistic and lack depth. They touch on convenience, cost, and government overreach but fail to articulate sophisticated reasoning about individual liberty, market principles, or comprehensive policy analysis. Arguments are more like complaints than structured policy positions. Missing key economic and philosophical foundations that underpin opposition to such regulations.
